steps.title: Simplify the radicals
steps.content:
Since $x>1$, use fractional exponents directly:
$$\sqrt[5]{4^5x^{40}}=4x^8$$
and
$$\sqrt[8]{3^8x}=3x^{\frac{1}{8}}$$

steps.title: Combine powers of x
steps.content:
Substitute the simplified radicals into the expression:
$$5(4x^8)(3x^{\frac{1}{8}})=60x^{8+\frac{1}{8}}=60x^{\frac{65}{8}}$$
So $$a=60$$ and $$b=\frac{65}{8}$$
